rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STELLA DAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/15/20. Still no proposed order. [LRSF 14.6 A.] No original bank statement. [LRSF 14.62.] The I&A showed assets on the date of death appraised at $164,522.05; the accounting shows no starting assets. What happened to the other tangible personal property (items 20-23 on the I&A)? For post-deceased heirs, Probate Code Section 13100 affidavits must be filed by their successor(s)-in-interest. The proposed distribution should be calculated on the full value of assets on hand, including the value of the tangible personal property that is proposed to be distributed to the administrator, which value will reduce the amount of cash to him. The 7/27/16 order authorized the administrator to pay Munros a retainer of approximately $5,200 but ordered that no additional fees shall be incurred without prior Court approval; the accounting shows a total of $11,053.67 paid to that firm plus $15,981.67 for bond. The attorney costs for Bowles & Verna LLP are not sufficiently described for the Court to determine if they are correct; see costs attached to David Hines's declaration, which clearly sets forth the payees and descriptions. This Court does not ordinarily permit reimbursement for travel to and from the court for hearings.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BOOKER T. FOGGY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing; however, it is noted that the heirs are not limited to the children of the decedent, as the predeceased wife reportedly died not more than 15 years before him, owning an interest in real property that passed to him, and no Reappraisal for Sale was filed concurrently with the Report of Status filed on 7/10/20, as alleged in paragraph 11 of the petition. The order appointing the administrator was filed on 10/4/18 but it was dated 10/3/18. The Allowance or Rejection of the creditor's claim, filed on 7/16/20, shows the total amount of the claim as $3,700,000. Status of closing the estate is set for 10/12/2021. If a petition for final distribution or a further report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is not filed by 10/5/2021, an appearance will be required on 10/12/2021.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SARAH MADELINE VARON
rulings.Calendar Matter: FUTURE ACCOUNTING
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): Conservator has not filed 1st account and status report. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WHITEY SHIRO LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/2020. 1) No proposed orders have been received. 2) Notice must be on a mandatory Judicial Council form (DE-115) meeting the requirements of Probate Code section 851(c). 3) An amendment to the petition or amended petition addressing the following issues must be filed and served: A) A complete copy of the trust instrument must be provided. B) Did the settlor leave any will? If so, please provide a copy of the will. C) The names and addresses of all persons who would be intestate heirs must be identified. D) A copy of the deed showing how the settlor held legal title at the time of his death must be provided. E) Has Carolyn Shiro-McLoughlin's resignation notice been given to all beneficiaries in accordance with Section 3.01 of the trust? F) The prayer for relief includes a determination of the validity of the trust, but neither the petition caption nor the notice of hearing include this relief. CRC 7.50 & 7.102. G) Why was notice to Jocelyn Shiro given to the P.O. Box address? Probate Code sections 1220(a)(3) & 17100. It should be noted that the proof of service on file does not show the date of mailing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES HAROLD ADAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/15/20. The Notice of Petition for Administer Estate does not reflect that the hearing is to admit a LOST will. Item 3d(6) of the petition shows the net value of real property as $250,000, but item 3d(4) shows the value as "TBD" and no encumbrances. Item 3g(2) of the petition should not be completed if item 3g(1) is. Item 5a(2) of the petition is incomplete. Item 8 of the petition is incomplete (not all persons mentioned in the will are listed) and does not indicate the person's relationship to the decedent. The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) is also missing from the list. Item 8 must clearly identify the trustee of the devisee trust and, if it is the petitioner, must clearly identify all of the trust beneficiaries. The date of death of any deceased heir or beneficiary must be set forth. The will is incomplete at Article Three (page 3): no personal representative (or alternate) is named. Did the testator leave a "Personal Property Memorandum"? A courtesy copy of any responsive papers and corrected proof of service must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/8/20.
